business_review.criterion: AC1: Xray scheduled reindexing fires correctly for datasources with a valid setting_id
business_review.notes: reindex_xray fetches Xray credentials via SettingsService.get_xray_creds with setting_id, constructs XrayDatasourceProcessor, and calls datasource_concurrency_manager.run(processor.incremental_reindex). validate_datasource ensures setting_id is present before job is scheduled.

business_review.criterion: AC2: SharePoint scheduled reindexing fires correctly for datasources with auth_type=='integration'
business_review.notes: reindex_sharepoint correctly executes for integration-auth SharePoint datasources. No explicit precondition guard restricts scheduling to auth_type=='integration' only; however, SharePointDatasourceProcessor._create_or_update_scheduler already prevents scheduler records from being created for OAuth types, so only integration-auth datasources can have scheduler settings in practice.

business_review.criterion: AC3: validate_datasource returns non-None for knowledge_base_xray and knowledge_base_sharepoint
business_review.notes: 'knowledge_base_xray' and 'knowledge_base_sharepoint' added to the allowlist in utils.py. Two tests confirm the positive path.

business_review.criterion: AC4: __schedule_datasource_job schedules APScheduler jobs for Xray and SharePoint
business_review.notes: Two new elif branches added in cron.py dispatch to reindex_xray and reindex_sharepoint respectively. Tests verify scheduler.add_job is called with the correct actor function.

business_review.criterion: AC5: Existing datasource types (Jira, Confluence, Git, etc.) not regressed
business_review.notes: All pre-existing branches in validate_datasource and __schedule_datasource_job are unchanged. All 119 trigger tests pass.

standards_review.notes: No commits made yet; format will be enforced at commit time.

standards_review.standard: code-quality: type hints on public functions
standards_review.notes: New functions reindex_xray and reindex_sharepoint follow existing actor signatures (no explicit return type, consistent with reindex_jira, reindex_confluence, etc.).
Additions follow existing code style; no obviously lint-failing patterns introduced.

standards_review.standard: security: no credentials, tokens, or secrets in code
standards_review.notes: Credentials fetched at runtime from SettingsService, not hardcoded.

rationale: All three review lenses ran successfully. The blind lens raised 5 items, all of which were dismissed after verification: logging IS present before the early returns (the blind lens misread the diff); ValueError catch is consistent with the existing _resume_sharepoint pattern; the `if not sp_index_info` guard covers the None case; hardcoded defaults match _resume_sharepoint; and datasource_concurrency_manager.run() is never wrapped in try/except in any existing actor. The edge-case lens raised 6 items: EC-001 (empty JQL fallback) is identical to the pattern already used in _resume_xray; EC-002 (ValueError) is dismissed as above; EC-003/EC-004 (OAuth SharePoint scheduling) is deferred — OAuth types cannot have scheduler settings created through normal flows because _create_or_update_scheduler blocks it at the API layer; EC-005 is a structural observation not a defect; EC-006 (stamp before guard) is consistent with all existing actors. The acceptance lens confirmed AC1, AC3, AC4, AC5 pass and AC2 is partial, but the auth_type constraint is not an explicit Jira AC — it is an implementation constraint that is enforced upstream. No blocking findings survived triage. Confidence is medium (not high) due to the AC2 partial and the deferred EC-003 edge case.
